company began to look them over.
Soon Secretary Blocker and Assist
ant Superintendent Wilson, accom-
panied by a policeman, came and
demanded their badges. The
strikers refused to surrender them
until given receipt, in order to be
able to collect their wages without
dispute. When the receipts were
given and the badges taken they
were ordered on their way.


Union men are pointing out that
all the assaults so far reported or
attempted have been made by COM
pany employees. 1t is claimed that
Lee Atkinson, an employe, was ar
rested for striking a man with a
slung shot, and that two other en-
ployes drew guns and threatened
to shoot They say they have the
evidence to prove this.


Two other assaults have been
charged to company men lately. one
occurring this morning at the post
office corner A bystander called
"scab" at a carman, who jumped off
and hammered the shouter. The
carman was big and the shouter
small. A special saw the proceed
ings and walked off.


]. J. Reardon, conductor on the
Liberty park line, Is reported as
beating up a man who called him
"scab" yesterday. A number OF
smaller scraps are reported today.
